title: Automated processing systems, apparatus, methods and related devices
abstract: Systems, apparatus, devices and methods for automated processing of nucleic acids and other samples using a container assembly with a plunger support member comprising at least one plunger extending a distance below the plunger support member, a vessel member comprising at least one vessel residing under the plunger support member, and a flexible impermeable barrier comprising an outer wall with an upper perimeter portion sealably attached to the plunger support member. The outer wall of the flexible barrier merges into a closed bottom surface defining a segment of a bottom of the container assembly. The bottom surface extends laterally a distance to define an inner flexible barrier segment that is sealably attached to the vessel member to define an enclosed interior chamber that resides under the plunger support member inside the flexible barrier with the at least one vessel with the liquid cavity residing inside the interior chamber.
claims:
Claims THAT WHICH IS CLAIMED: 1. An automated sample processing system, comprising: a housing having at least one processing workstation; a container assembly residing in the workstation, the container assembly comprising: a plunger support member comprising at least one plunger extending a distance below the plunger support member; a vessel member comprising at least one vessel with a liquid cavity sized and configured to hold liquid therein residing under the plunger support member; a flexible barrier that is sealably attached to the plunger support member and the vessel member to define a sealed interior chamber that resides between the plunger support member and the vessel member inside the flexible barrier with the at least one vessel with the liquid cavity residing inside the sealed interior chamber; and a robot held at the workstation in communication with the container assembly, the robot configured to move one or both of the vessel member and the plunger support member up and down and laterally while the flexible barrier remains sealably attached to the vessel member and the plunger support member, wherein the flexible barrier has a longitudinally extending side wall that changes in length during operation based on the robot movement. 2. The system of Claim 1, wherein the flexible barrier comprises a longitudinally extending outer sidewall with an upper perimeter portion sealably attached to the plunger support member and a longitudinally extending inner sidewall attached to the vessel member with a bridging closed bottom segment extending laterally therebetween, and wherein the bridging closed bottom segment defines a segment of a bottom of the container assembly. 3. The system of Claim 1, wherein the at least one vessel of the vessel device comprises first, second and third vessels residing adjacent each other. 4. The system of Claim 3, wherein at least one of the vessels has a different shape and size from at least one other vessel. 5. The system of Claim 1, wherein one or both of the vessel member and the plunger support member moves up and down between 1-10 inches and laterally front to back, side to side or both front to back and side to side, between 2-6 inches. 6. The system of Claim 2, wherein the longitudinally extending inner and outer sidewalls of the flexible barrier extend on opposing sides of an internal annular gap space residing therebetween, over the bridging closed bottom segment of the flexible barrier. 7. The system of Claim 1 , wherein the robot comprises a robotic arm with a head, and wherein the system further comprises: a vessel support member that is external to the container assembly and resides under and releasably receives the vessel member of the container assembly; and a flexible boot that extends below the vessel support member over the robotic head, and wherein the vessel support member comprises at least one vacuum port that supplies a vacuum to pull the container assembly against the vessel support member. 8. The system of Claim 7, wherein the vessel support member comprises a circumferentially extending collar that radially extends a distance beyond vessel supports of the vessel support member. 9. The system of Claim 1 , wherein the plunger support member is held stationary in the workstation during operation while the flexible barrier and the vessel member move relative to the plunger support member to position the at least one plunger in and out of the at least one vessel of the vessel member and while the system is in a closed state to thereby provide aseptic processing. 10. The system of Claim 1, further comprising an air management system in fluid communication with the sealed interior chamber, the air management system comprising a bladder connected to first and second air conduits attached to respective ports on the plunger support member and a pinch valve in communication with the first and second conduits to control flow of air into and out of the container assembly. 11. The system of Claim 1 , wherein the at least one plunger of the plunger support member comprises a mixing plunger and an elution plunger, and wherein the plungers have an open top and a closed inwardly extending cavity. 12. The system of Claim 1, wherein the at least one vessel of the vessel member comprises a plurality of spaced apart vessels, each with an upwardly facing fluid cavity, and wherein the plunger support member comprises first and second downwardly extending pipettes that are sized and configured to concurrently enter aligned ones of the vessels. 13. The system of Claim 1, wherein the housing comprises a vertical panel with bag attachment members for suspending bags of input liquids and a plurality of peristaltic pumps under the bag attachment members. 14. The system of Claim 13, further comprising conduit tensioner members attached to the vertical panel residing under the peristaltic pumps and configured to direct respective conduits down and outward toward a bubble sensor assembly and into channels held by or formed in the plunger support member with the conduits held in tension as they enter the bubble sensor assembly. 15. The system of Claim 1 , further comprising a magnet held on a shaft at the workstation that is extendable and retractable into the at least one plunger held by the plunger support member to externally interact with magnetic beads in the cavity of the at least one vessel of the vessel member. 16. The system of Claim 1 , further comprising a datum head configured to have a retracted configuration and an extended configuration aligned with a respective container assembly at a respective workstation, wherein the datum head comprises a magnet guide, a magnet held on a shaft that is extendable and retractable, suction cups that attach to flat surfaces on the plunger support member, and locator pins that enter locator pin channels with closed lower ends held by the plunger support member. 17. The system of Claim 16, wherein the magnet guide comprises an open through channel for allowing the magnet and shaft to slide therethrough, and pivoting clamp members, wherein the at least one plunger comprises a mixing plunger with an open top, 

 and a cavity with a closed lower end, and wherein the magnet guide is configured to enter the top of the mixing plunger and the pivoting clamp members are configured to pivot to press a wall of the mixing plunger against the magnet guide thereby clamping in place. 18. The system of Claim 1 , further comprising an aliquot tray comprising a seal bar window, a plurality of bubble sensor windows, a plurality of pinch valve windows and a conduit manifold that are in fluid communication with an aliquot conduit and aliquot containers, and wherein the housing comprises a vertical panel that includes a seal bar that resides in the seal bar window, bubble sensors that reside in the bubble sensor windows and pinch valves that reside in the pinch valve windows when a respective tray is attached to the vertical panel. 19. The system of Claim 1 , further comprising a bubble sensor assembly with a rectangular lid with a perimeter long side and a short side, with the lid pivotably attached along one short side, the bubble sensor assembly further comprising a plurality of laterally spaced apart bubble sensors sized and shaped to hold a conduit segment with the lid closed thereover. 20. The system of Claim 1, further comprising an aliquot peristaltic pump held by the housing downstream of the workstation above an aliquot tray holding an aliquot manifold of tubing and a plurality of aliquot cassettes with a respective coil of tubing held inside the cassettes in fluid communication with tubing attached to the peristaltic pump. 21. A processing apparatus, comprising: a housing comprising a first vertical wall panel and a second vertical wall panel, a workstation with an isolation container assembly mounting bracket extending between the first and second vertical wall panels; a plurality of bag attachment members held by the first vertical wall panel; a plurality of peristaltic pumps held by the first vertical wall panel below the bag attachment members; a robotic arm held by the housing to be able to vertically extend and retract and travel laterally at the at least one workstation; and 

 a tray holding segment held by the second vertical wall panel, wherein the tray holding segment comprises a tube sealer, a plurality of pinch valves, a plurality of bubble sensors and a door that closes over the tube sealer. 22. The apparatus of Claim 21, further comprising: a datum head held by the housing over the mounting bracket at the workstation; and a peristaltic pump held by the second vertical wall panel. 23. The apparatus of Claim 21, wherein the datum head is extendable and retractable from a protruding circular housing segment residing above the isolation container assembly mounting bracket. 24. The apparatus of Claim 22, wherein the datum head has a retracted configuration and an extended configuration and, during operation, is aligned with a respective container assembly held on the mounting bracket at the workstation, wherein the datum head comprises a magnet guide, a magnet held on a shaft, suction cups and locator pins, wherein the magnet guide, magnet, suction cups and locator pins are extendable and retractable when the datum head is in the extended configuration. 25. The apparatus of Claim 24, wherein the magnet guide comprises an open through channel for allowing the magnet and shaft to slide therethrough, and pivoting clamp members, wherein the at least one plunger comprises a mixing plunger with an open top and a cavity with a closed lower end, and wherein the magnet guide is configured to enter the top of the mixing plunger and the pivoting clamp members are configured to pivot to press a wall of the mixing plunger against the magnet guide. 26. The apparatus of Claim 21 , further comprising a flexible boot that is sealably attached to a collar extending about a perimeter of an open space at the workstation that resides under the mounting bracket and that encases the robotic arm. 27. The apparatus of Claim 26, wherein the flexible boot comprises a rigid or semi-rigid vessel support member on a top portion thereof sealably attached to the flexible boot. 28. The apparatus of Claim 27, wherein the vessel support member comprises a plurality of adjacent vessel supports, each with a longitudinally extending cavity, and wherein the vessel support member comprises at least one vacuum port that, in operation, applies a vacuum to a vessel member of an isolation container and a circumferentially extending collar that radially extends a distance outside the vessel support member. 29. The apparatus of Claim 21 , further comprising conduit tensioner members residing under the peristaltic pumps held by the first vertical wall panel and configured to direct respective conduits down and outward toward the workstation. 30. The apparatus of Claim 21, further comprising a bubble sensor assembly with a rectangular lid with a perimeter long side and a short side residing adjacent the workstation, with the lid pivotably attached along one short side, the bubble sensor assembly further comprising a plurality of laterally spaced apart bubble sensors sized and shaped to hold a conduit segment with the lid closed thereover. 31. The apparatus of Claim 21 , wherein the bag attachment members can be automatically and selectively illuminated, and wherein the apparatus is configured with a processor that illuminates the bag attachment members in a defined sequence to facilitate assembly of bags to the bag attachment members in a desired order. 32. The apparatus of Claim 21 , further comprising a calibration jig the calibration jig comprising cooperating first and second members that have datum pins facing each other that contact to define a datum location. 33. An isolation container assembly, comprising: a rigid or semi-rigid plunger support member comprising a horizontally extending wall with a thickness, at least one plunger extending downwardly below the wall, at least one pipette shell extending downwardly below the wall and surrounding pipette tubing extending therethrough, an air entry port and an air exit port extending through the wall, and a plurality of sealed liquid entry ports extending through the wall; a rigid or semi-rigid vessel member comprising a plurality of mixing vessels, each with a liquid cavity, residing under the plunger support member; and 

 a flexible barrier having an upper outer wall portion that is sealably attached to the plunger support member and an inner segment sealably attached to the vessel member to define an outer wall and bottom segment of the container assembly and provide an enclosed interior chamber with the vessels of the vessel member residing in the interior chamber. 34. The container assembly of Claim 33, wherein the inner segment of the flexible barrier and the vessel member can travel up and down and laterally with respect to the plunger support member while retaining a closed state of the container assembly to thereby allow aseptic processing. 35. The container assembly of Claim 33, wherein the plurality of sealed liquid entry ports includes a bank of liquid entry ports residing proximate a plurality of conduit channels that merge toward each other on the external surface proximate the bank of liquid entry ports. 36. The container assembly of Claim 33, further comprising an extension shelf residing off one side of the plunger support member a distance beyond the outer wall of the flexible barrier, wherein the extension shelf comprises a plurality of adjacent parallel channels, each sized and configured to receive a conduit of a fluid input. 37. The container assembly of Claim 33, wherein the vessel member comprises an outer wall that is spaced apart a distance from a neighboring vessel held by the vessel member, wherein the vessel member comprises an open bottom cavity residing under the vessels, and wherein the inner segment of the flexible barrier is sealably attached to an upper portion of the outer wall of the vessel member. 38. The container assembly of Claim 33, wherein the flexible barrier comprises longitudinally extending inner and outer sidewalls extending on opposing sides of a bridging closed bottom segment, and wherein the inner and outer sidewalls of the flexible barrier extend on opposing sides of an internal annular gap space residing therebetween, over the bridging closed bottom segment of the flexible barrier, and wherein the bridging closed bottom segment defines a portion of a bottom of the container assembly with the vessel member defining the medial portion of the bottom of the container assembly. 39. A container device for an isolation container assembly, comprising: a rigid or semi-rigid plunger support member comprising a horizontally extending primary wall with a thickness, at least one plunger extending downwardly below the wall, at least one pipette shell extending downwardly below the wall and surrounding pipette tubing extending therethrough, first and second ports extending through the wall, and a plurality of sealed liquid entry ports extending through the wall. 40. The container device of Claim 39, wherein the primary wall further comprises a plurality of conduit channels extending from one side toward some of the liquid entry ports. 41. The container device of Claim 39, further comprising an extension shelf residing off one side of the plunger support member with a plurality of adjacent conduit channels and at least one mounting feature with an open upwardly facing aperture. 42. The container device of Claim 39, wherein the primary wall merges into upwardly extending arcuate sidewalls extending above the primary wall along a perimeter segment thereof and the primary wall comprises a plurality of upwardly extending closed projections with circular perimeters that route tubing during shipment. 43. An outlet device for an automated biological processing apparatus, comprising: an aliquot tray comprising an elongate window, a plurality of bubble sensor windows residing below the window, a plurality of pinch valve windows residing above the window, and a plurality of aliquot receptacles attached to a conduit manifold. 44. The device of Claim 43, further comprising right and left side handles attached to the tray and locator pin apertures extending through the tray, one above another, on opposing sides of the tray. 45. The device of Claim 43, further comprising a plurality of side-by-side receptacle channels residing in an outer facing surface of the tray above the heat seal window. 46. The device of Claim 43, wherein the receptacle channels define rectangular pockets for holding rectangular cassettes with tubing attached to the conduit manifold. 47. A method of processing a sample, comprising: providing an isolation container assembly, wherein the isolation container assembly comprises a flexible barrier attached to (i) a rigid or semi-rigid plunger support member holding at least one plunger and/or at least one pipette and (ii) a rigid or semi-rigid vessel member holding at least one vessel comprising liquid and being spaced apart from and beneath the plunger support member whereby the flexible barrier defines an outer wall and a bottom segment of the isolation container assembly; and automatically moving at least one of the vessel member or the plunger support member up and down, side to side and/or front to back while retaining a closed state of the isolation container assembly thereby allowing for aseptic processing of the sample. 48. The method of Claim 47, wherein the at least one plunger comprises at least one plunger that has a downwardly extending wall that surrounds a cavity and provides a closed internal channel, the method further comprises selectively directing the at least one plunger of the plunger support member to enter at least one vessel, then extending a magnet down into the plunger to reside proximate a lower end thereof. 49. The method of Claim 48, wherein, after the magnet is extended, the moving is carried out to move the vessel member up and down relative to the plunger with the magnet therein to cause the magnet to interact with magnetic beads in liquid in the at least one vessel. 50. The method of Claim 47, wherein, during the moving, the plunger support member remains stationary and attached to the flexible barrier while a longitudinally extending wall of the flexible barrier changes in longitudinal length. 51. The method of Claim 47, wherein, before the providing step, calibrating a datum location using a calibration jig attached to a datum head that is external to the isolation container assembly to align plungers to the at least one vessel of the vessel member. 52. The method of Claim 47, wherein the moving is carried out to engage the at least one plunger with an aligned vessel of the at least one vessel, then away from the . 

 plunger, then engages a pipette or a reagent entry port(s) on or in the plunger support member. 53. The method of Claim 47, wherein the moving is carried out to move the vessel member (i) up to engage the at least one plunger then (ii) down for clearance, then (iii) laterally, front to back and/or side to side, away from the plunger, then (iv) up again to engage a pipette or a reagent entry port(s) on or in the plunger support member while the plunger support member remains stationary. 54. The method of Claim 47, wherein the plunger support member includes first and second ports, the first port attached to a first end of a length of a first air conduit and the second port attached to a first end of a length of a second air conduit, the first and second conduits having a second opposing end that is attached to a bladder, and wherein the method further comprises circulating air to and from the container assembly and the bladder using a pinch valve in communication with both the first and second conduits to control flow of air into and out of the isolation container assembly. 55. The method of Claim 47, wherein the vessel member includes a plurality of adjacent vessel members and the plunger support comprises a plurality of spaced apart plungers and a plurality of spaced apart pipettes all extending downward a distance under the plunger support member, and wherein the moving is carried out to concurrently engage different plungers or different pipettes. 56. The method of Claim 47, further comprising withdrawing elution from one of the at least one vessel in the container assembly into an aliquot output flow conduit and directing the elution into aliquot receptacles. 57. The method of Claim 47, further comprising monitoring a proximity sensor signal of a proximity sensor aligned with rollers of an aliquot peristaltic pump in communication with the aliquot output flow conduit to time aspiration from a home position of a roller of the pump to deliver aliquot amounts of processed sample to aliquot containers from conduit extending from the isolation container to an aliquot receptacle. 58. The method of Claim 47, wherein the moving is carried out to cause (a) mixing of liquid between the plunger and at least one of the at least one vessel and/or (b) separation of magnetic beads from liquid in at least one of the at least one vessel. 59. The method of Claim 47, further comprising closing first and second conduits connecting a bladder to the isolation container assembly, and performing a seal integrity check based on pressure measurements from a pressure sensor in fluid communication with the isolation container assembly. 60. The method of Claim 47, wherein the sample is a patient sample comprising RNA. 61. The method of Claim 47, wherein the sample comprises tumor homogenate. 62. The method of Claim 48, wherein the at least one vessel comprises a vessel with a solution of magnetic beads, the method further comprising removing the plunger from the vessel with the solution of magnetic beads to form a layer of liquid comprising magnetic beads extending over a surface area of the plunger and exposing the layer of liquid with the magnetic beads on the plunger to gas in the container assembly to thereby dry the layer of liquid with the beads after the withdrawal from the vessel with the solution without requiring heating of the plunger and/or beads. 63. A medical device, comprising: a housing; and a coil comprising a plurality of stacked layers of tubing in the housing, the coil of tubing having opposing first and second ends, each of the first and second ends attached to a respective length of conduit with a length sufficient to extend out of the housing. 64. The device of Claim 63, wherein the housing comprises at least one pivoting cover that can open and close to expose an interior of the housing with at least one attachment member, wherein the device further comprises a support body holding the coil of tubing attached to the attachment member. 65. The device of Claim 64, wherein the support body has an open center space and an inner wall that engages the attachment member. 66. The device of Claim 63, further comprising a support body overmolded to the coil, wherein the support body with the coil is held in the housing. 67. The device of Claim 63, wherein the plurality of layers is between 2-15 layers, and wherein the coil has an inner diameter that is between 0.5 inches and 3 inches. 68. The device of Claim 63, wherein the coil tubing is medical grade polypropylene tubing, wherein the conduit comprises a medical grade thermoplastic elastomer, wherein the first and second ends of tubing extending out of the housing are sealed and the tubing holds a pharmaceutical medication or ingredient thereof. 69. The device of Claim 63, wherein the housing comprises at least one pivoting outer cover that can open and close to expose an interior of the housing with at least one attachment member and an end with a conduit egress aperture, wherein the device further comprises an overmolded support body holding the coil of tubing in the housing attached to the attachment member, wherein the support body comprises an outerwall with an arcuate or circular shape, wherein the attached lengths of conduit are configured to wrap against the outer wall of the support body in the housing and allow the at least one outer cover to pivot shut to enclose the coil of tubing and the conduit, and wherein the coil of tubing is configured to hold between 1-5000 Î¼Î, of liquid. 70. The device of Claim 63, further comprising a vaccine held in the tubing of the coil. 71. The device of Claims 63, further comprising human cells held in the tubing of the coil. 72. The device of Claim 63, further comprising at least one of the following held in the coil tubing: antigen presenting cells; 

 dendritic cells, optionally the dendritic cells are pulsed with one or more antigens or pulsed with RNA encoding one or more antigens and optionally the antigen is a tumor- specific antigen or a pathogen-specific antigen and optionally the pathogen can be HIV or HCV; nucleic acid; and RNA cells. 73. The device of Claim 63, further comprising a luer lock on an end portion of each respective length of the conduit. 74. An automated sample processing system, comprising: a housing having at least one processing workstation; an isolation container assembly residing in the workstation, the isolation container assembly comprising a flexible impermeable barrier sealably attached to at least one rigid member of the container assembly and cooperating with the at least one rigid member to define an enclosed internal chamber, a plurality of tubing lines in fluid communication with an internal chamber of the sealed chamber, first and second conduits in fluid communication with the internal chamber and extending to a bladder having a gas capacity of between 5-10 liters, and at least one pinch valve configured to selectively shut and open the first and second conduits to control gas flow to and from the bladder; and a robot in communication with the isolation container assembly that is configured to move at least one component of the isolation container assembly to vary an internal volume of the internal chamber by 1-10 liters while the flexible barrier remains sealably attached to the rigid member so that the internal chamber remains enclosed. 75. The system of Claim 74, wherein the at least one pinch valve is a single pinch valve that is configured to concurrently open and concurrently close the first and second conduits. 76. The system of Claim 74, wherein the at least one rigid member comprises a plunger support member comprising at least one plunger extending a distance below the plunger support member; and a vessel member comprising at least one vessel with a liquid cavity sized and configured to hold liquid therein residing under the plunger support member; 

 wherein the flexible impermeable barrier comprises an outer wall with an upper perimeter portion sealably attached to the plunger support member, wherein the outer wall merges into a closed bottom surface defining a segment of a bottom of the container assembly, and wherein the bottom surface extends laterally a distance to define an inner flexible barrier segment that is sealably attached to the vessel member to define the enclosed internal chamber with the at least one vessel with the liquid cavity residing inside the internal chamber. 77. The system of Claim 74, wherein the robot is in communication with the vessel member and is configured to move one or both of the vessel member or the plunger support member up and down between 1-10 inches and laterally front to back, side to side or both front to back and side to side between 2-6 inches while the flexible barrier remains sealably attached to the vessel member and the plunger support member so that the internal chamber remains enclosed. 78. A method of controlling pumping of a peristaltic pump, comprising: providing a peristaltic pump comprising a roller assembly with plurality of circumferentially spaced apart rollers that rotate about a fixed point; detecting an electrical signal from a proximity sensor under the fixed point when a roller is aligned over the proximity sensor; identifying a position of the roller using the electrical signal; and starting a pumping action for liquid in a conduit extending about the roller assembly from the identified position to thereby improve an accuracy of a pumping operation, 79. A peristaltic pump comprising: a housing; a roller assembly in the housing with a plurality of circumferentially spaced apart rollers that rotate about a fixed point; a proximity sensor in the housing under the fixed point configured to generate an electrical signal that can be used to identify when a roller is aligned over the proximity sensor; and at least one processor in communication with the proximity sensor configured to identifying a position of the roller using the electrical signal and start a pumping action for 

 liquid in a conduit extending about the roller assembly to thereby improve accuracy of aspiration volume delivered to aliquot vessels based on a pumping operation. 80. The pump of Claim 79, further comprising a cover attached to the housing over the roller assembly to provide an arcuate gap over the roller assembly with a gap space sufficiently small to inhibit pinch points for fingers. 81. A set of disposable processing components of an isolation container system in a sterile package, comprising: (i) an isolation container assembly comprising: a rigid or semi-rigid support member, a vessel member comprising a plurality of spaced apart vessels that can hold liquid therein, and a flexible barrier having an upper outer portion that is sealably attached to the support member and an inner portion attached to the vessel member to define an interior chamber therebetween configured to allow the flexible barrier to move to change a volume of the interior chamber while remaining sealed to the support member and the vessel member, the support member comprising a plurality of adjacent parallel horizontally extending channels on an upper surface thereof; a bladder in fluid communication with the sealed interior chamber via pre- attached first and second conduits attached to the support member on one end and the bladder on an opposing end; and a plurality of input tubing lines pre-attached to the isolation container assembly to be in fluid communication with the sealed interior chamber, each held by a respective channel on the upper surface of the support member, the input tubing lines each having a key member attached thereto; and (ii) an aliquot tray comprising a plurality of aliquot vessels with attached tubing held by the tray. 82. The set of components of Claim 81, wherein the tray is detachably held by the support member in the sterile package. 83. The set of components of Claim 81, further comprising a plurality of chimney projections spaced apart about the upper surface of the support member with the input tubing lines routed thereabout, wherein a length of the input tubing lines are configured to 

 be removed from the chimneys and routed through the channels to position free ends thereof outside a side of the boundary of the support member when attached to respective input liquid tubing.
